title: Pludux Backtest Strategy Schema

version.title: Schema Version
version.description: Version of the Pludux strategy schema. Must be 2 for this draft.

series.title: Named Series
series.description: A dictionary of reusable named series. Each key must be lower_snake_case. Define indicators, transformed price series, and intermediate calculations here so positions, signals, and plots can reference them consistently. When one named series needs the numeric output of another named series, use SERIES_VALUE. When a method needs the named series definition itself, use SERIES_NODE.

long.title: Long Side
long.description: Rules for opening and closing long positions. Set to false to disable long trading.

short.title: Short Side
short.description: Rules for opening and closing short positions. Set to false to disable short trading.

positions.title: Positions
positions.description: Trading rules for long and short positions. Each side can be disabled with false, or configured with entry and optional exit signals.

trailing.title: Trailing
trailing.description: When true, the stop loss trails favorable price movement instead of staying fixed.

oneOf.title: Stop Loss Settings
oneOf.description: Object form of the global stop-loss configuration.

stopLoss.title: Stop Loss
stopLoss.description: Global stop-loss behavior for the strategy. Use false to disable it, or provide an object to enable it and optionally make it trailing.

rMultiple.title: R Multiple
rMultiple.description: Profit target measured in multiples of 1R, where 1R is the strategy's risk distance.

oneOf.title: Take Profit Settings
oneOf.description: Object form of the global take-profit configuration.

takeProfit.title: Take Profit
takeProfit.description: Global take-profit behavior for the strategy. Use false to disable it, or provide an object to enable it and define the target in R-multiples.

plots.title: Plots
plots.description: Optional visual plot groups for chart rendering. Plot groups can be overlaid on price or rendered in their own pane.

signal.title: Signal
signal.description: Boolean signal definition for this entry rule. It can be an inline signal object or a literal true/false value.

entry.title: Entry
entry.description: Entry rule for this side. The entry signal decides when to open a position.

signal.title: Signal
signal.description: Boolean signal definition for this exit rule. It can be an inline signal object or a literal true/false value.

exit.title: Exit
exit.description: Optional exit rule for this side. The exit signal decides when to close a position before stop-loss or take-profit rules.

positionSide.title: Position Side
positionSide.description: Configuration for one trading direction, such as long or short. Use false to disable the side, or provide entry and optional exit signal rules.

seriesAsParameter.title: Series Input (Flexible Expression)
seriesAsParameter.description:
Represents an input that can be a constant, a raw price series, a previously defined named series, or an inline series expression.

Authoring preference (IMPORTANT for AI generation):
1. Prefer using SERIES_VALUE to reference a named series defined in top-level 'series'.
2. Prefer full method objects (e.g., EMA, RSI) when parameters are customized.
3. Use quote shorthand (e.g., "CLOSE") only for raw OHLCV inputs.
4. Use indicator shorthand (e.g., "EMA") ONLY when the default parameters are intended exactly.
5. Use plain numbers only for constant values.

Consistency rule:
- Prefer defining reusable logic in top-level 'series' and referencing it via SERIES_VALUE instead of repeating inline expressions.

Discouraged but valid:
- Mixing multiple styles in the same strategy (e.g., sometimes using shorthand, sometimes full objects).
- Using indicator shorthand when custom parameters are needed.

This flexibility exists for convenience, but AI-generated strategies should follow a consistent style using named series and explicit method definitions.

oneOf.description: Quote shorthand representing raw market data. Preferred only for direct price/volume inputs.
A full series expression using method + params. Preferred when defining indicators or transformations.
Indicator shorthand with default parameters. Convenience syntax only. Avoid using this if any parameter customization is required.

examples.description: Preferred: raw price input using quote shorthand
Preferred: referencing a previously defined series
Preferred: explicit indicator with custom parameters
Allowed but discouraged unless default parameters are intended exactly

method.title: Method
method.description: Uppercase method name that identifies what this object does, such as SMA, RSI, CROSSOVER, or LINE.

params.title: Parameters
params.description: Method-specific parameters. The required keys depend on the method.

baseMethod.title: Base Method Object
baseMethod.description: Base shape shared by Pludux method objects. Most series, signals, and plots are expressed as an object with a UPPERCASE method name and an optional params object.

quoteSeries.title: Quote Series
quoteSeries.description: Built-in market data series from the current bar, such as OPEN, HIGH, LOW, CLOSE, or VOLUME.

method.title: Method
method.description: Always use SERIES_NODE to refer to a named series definition itself.

name.title: Series Name
name.description: Name of a previously defined series in the top-level series object.

seriesNodeSeries.title: Series Node
seriesNodeSeries.description: References a previously defined named series as a series node. Use SERIES_NODE when another method needs the named series definition itself, such as selecting one output from a multi-output indicator. Do not use SERIES_NODE where a numeric value is expected.

method.title: Method
method.description: Always use SERIES_VALUE to read the numeric output of a named series.

name.title: Series Name
name.description: Name of a previously defined series in the top-level series object.

seriesValueSeries.title: Series Value
seriesValueSeries.description: References the evaluated output value of a previously defined named series at the current bar. Use SERIES_VALUE when a method needs a numeric series value, such as comparisons, arithmetic, and crossover signals.

output.title: Output Name
output.description: Which output line to select from the source series, such as upper-band, lower-band, macd-line, signal-line, or histogram.

source.title: Source Series
source.description: Source multi-output series node. Use a multi-output indicator directly, or use SERIES_NODE to refer to a named multi-output series. Do not use SERIES_VALUE here because SELECT_OUTPUT needs the series node, not its evaluated numeric value.

selectOutputSeries.title: Select Output
selectOutputSeries.description: Selects one named output from a multi-output series node, such as Bollinger Bands, MACD, STOCH, or STOCH_RSI. The source should usually be a multi-output indicator definition directly, or a SERIES_NODE reference to a named multi-output series.

source.title: Source Series
source.description: Series to read from a previous bar.

period.title: Lookback Period
period.description: Number of bars to look back. 0 means the current bar, 1 means one bar ago, and so on.

lookbackSeries.title: Lookback
lookbackSeries.description: Returns the value of a source series from a previous bar. For example, period 1 means one bar ago.

value.title: Value
value.description: Constant numeric value to use as a series.

valueSeries.title: Value
valueSeries.description: Wraps a constant number as a series object.

field.title: Field Name
field.description: Name of the custom dataset field to read.

dataSeries.title: Data Field
dataSeries.description: Reads a custom data field from the input dataset, such as a user-provided column in the historical data file.

period.title: Period
period.description: Lookback period used by the indicator.

source.title: Source
source.description: Source series used as the indicator input.

smaSeries.title: Simple Moving Average
smaSeries.description: Calculates the simple moving average of a source series over a lookback period.

period.title: Period
period.description: Lookback period used by the indicator.

source.title: Source
source.description: Source series used as the indicator input.

emaSeries.title: Exponential Moving Average
emaSeries.description: Calculates the exponential moving average of a source series over a lookback period.

period.title: Period
period.description: Lookback period used by the indicator.

source.title: Source
source.description: Source series used as the indicator input.

rmaSeries.title: Running Moving Average
rmaSeries.description: Calculates Wilder's running moving average of a source series over a lookback period.

period.title: Period
period.description: Lookback period used by the indicator.

source.title: Source
source.description: Source series used as the indicator input.

wmaSeries.title: Weighted Moving Average
wmaSeries.description: Calculates the weighted moving average of a source series over a lookback period.

period.title: Period
period.description: Lookback period used by the indicator.

source.title: Source
source.description: Source series used as the indicator input.

hmaSeries.title: Hull Moving Average
hmaSeries.description: Calculates the Hull moving average of a source series over a lookback period.

maSeries.title: Moving Average Series
maSeries.description: Union of supported moving-average method objects.

period.title: Period
period.description: Lookback period used by the indicator.

source.title: Source
source.description: Source series used as the indicator input.

rsiSeries.title: Relative Strength Index
rsiSeries.description: Calculates the RSI oscillator from a source series.

maSmoothingType.title: Smoothing Type
maSmoothingType.description: Moving-average smoothing method used to smooth true range into ATR.

atrSeries.title: Average True Range
atrSeries.description: Calculates ATR as a volatility measure using a chosen smoothing method.

period.title: Period
period.description: Rolling window length for the standard deviation calculation.

source.title: Source
source.description: Source series whose dispersion is measured.

stddevSeries.title: Standard Deviation
stddevSeries.description: Calculates rolling standard deviation of a source series.

maType.title: Moving Average Type
maType.description: Moving average method used for the Bollinger Bands middle line.

period.title: Period
period.description: Lookback period for the middle band and standard deviation.

stddev.title: Standard Deviation Multiplier
stddev.description: Number of standard deviations used to place the upper and lower bands.

maSource.title: Moving Average Source
maSource.description: Source series used to calculate the Bollinger Bands.

bbSeries.title: Bollinger Bands
bbSeries.description: Calculates a multi-output Bollinger Bands series with upper, middle, and lower bands.

maSource.title: maSource
maSource.description: Source series used for the Keltner Channel center line.

maPeriod.title: maPeriod
maPeriod.description: Lookback period for the center-line moving average.

maMethodType.title: maMethodType
maMethodType.description: Moving average method used for the center line.

bandMethodType.title: bandMethodType
bandMethodType.description: Volatility method used for the channel width. ATR uses average true range, TR uses true range, and RangeHighLow uses raw high-low range.

bandAtrPeriod.title: bandAtrPeriod
bandAtrPeriod.description: ATR period used when bandMethodType is ATR.

multiplier.title: multiplier
multiplier.description: Multiplier applied to the band width.

kcSeries.title: Keltner Channel
kcSeries.description: Calculates a multi-output Keltner Channel series using a moving average center line and a volatility band method.

source.title: Source
source.description: Source series used for the MACD calculation.

macdSeries.title: MACD
macdSeries.description: Calculates a multi-output MACD series with MACD line, signal line, and histogram.

kPeriod.title: kPeriod
kPeriod.description: Lookback period used to compute raw %K.

high.title: high
high.description: Series used as the high input.

low.title: low
low.description: Series used as the low input.

close.title: close
close.description: Series used as the close input.

stochSeries.title: Stochastic Oscillator
stochSeries.description: Calculates a multi-output stochastic oscillator with %K and %D outputs.

kPeriod.title: kPeriod
kPeriod.description: Lookback period used to compute raw stochastic values on RSI.

rsiInput.title: rsiInput
rsiInput.description: Source series used to calculate RSI before applying stochastic.

stochRsiSeries.title: Stochastic RSI
stochRsiSeries.description: Calculates a multi-output stochastic RSI oscillator with %K and %D outputs.

augend.title: Augend
augend.description: First numeric input to add. Prefer SERIES_VALUE for named series and plain numbers for constants.

addend.title: Addend
addend.description: Second numeric input to add. Prefer SERIES_VALUE for named series and plain numbers for constants.

params.title: Parameters
params.description:
Parameters for addition.

Authoring preference (IMPORTANT for AI generation):
1. Prefer SERIES_VALUE for named series inputs.
2. Prefer plain numbers for constants.
3. Prefer naming reusable arithmetic expressions in top-level 'series' instead of repeating them inline.

addSeries.title: Add
addSeries.description: Returns the sum of two numeric inputs.

minuend.title: Minuend
minuend.description: Value to subtract from. Prefer SERIES_VALUE for named series.

subtrahend.title: Subtrahend
subtrahend.description: Value to subtract. Prefer SERIES_VALUE for named series and numbers for constants.

params.title: Parameters
params.description: Parameters for subtraction. Prefer reusable named series instead of repeated inline expressions.

subtractSeries.title: Subtract
subtractSeries.description: Returns the result of subtracting one numeric input from another.

params.title: Parameters
params.description: Parameters for multiplication. Prefer SERIES_VALUE for named series and plain numbers for constants such as 2, 0.5, or 100.

multiplySeries.title: Multiply
multiplySeries.description: Returns the product of two numeric inputs.

divisor.title: Divisor
divisor.description: Numeric value used as the divisor. Ensure the divisor is intended to be non-zero at runtime.

params.title: Parameters
params.description: Parameters for division. Prefer SERIES_VALUE for named series and plain numbers for constants.

divideSeries.title: Divide
divideSeries.description: Returns one numeric input divided by another.

operand.title: Operand
operand.description: Numeric value to multiply by -1. Prefer SERIES_VALUE for named series.

negateSeries.title: Negate
negateSeries.description: Returns the negative of a numeric input.

operand.title: Operand
operand.description: Numeric value whose square root is calculated.

params.title: Parameters
params.description: Parameters for square root. Use only when the runtime values are intended to be non-negative.

sqrtSeries.title: Square Root
sqrtSeries.description: Returns the square root of a numeric input.

source.title: Source
source.description: Numeric source whose bar-to-bar change is calculated. Prefer SERIES_VALUE for named series and quote shorthand for raw OHLCV inputs.

params.title: Parameters
params.description: Parameters for bar-to-bar change. Prefer defining reusable source logic in top-level 'series' when the same source is used in multiple places.

changeSeries.title: Change
changeSeries.description: Returns the bar-to-bar difference of a numeric source.

minuend.title: Minuend
minuend.description: First numeric input in the absolute-difference calculation.

subtrahend.title: Subtrahend
subtrahend.description: Second numeric input in the absolute-difference calculation.

params.title: Parameters
params.description: Parameters for absolute difference. Prefer SERIES_VALUE for named series and numbers for constants.

absDiffSeries.title: Absolute Difference
absDiffSeries.description: Returns the absolute value of the difference between two numeric inputs.

base.title: Base
base.description: Base numeric input that the percentage is applied to. Prefer quote shorthand for direct OHLCV input and SERIES_VALUE for named series.

percentage.title: Percentage
percentage.description: Percentage amount to apply, such as 2 for 2%.

params.title: Parameters
params.description: Parameters for percentage-of-base calculation. This is commonly used for offsets, thresholds, and derived levels.

percentageSeries.title: Percentage of Base
percentageSeries.description: Returns a percentage of a base numeric input.

series.title: Series Definition
series.description: Any valid Pludux series definition. This can be a raw market series, indicator, transformation, arithmetic series, SELECT_OUTPUT chain, SERIES_NODE reference, or SERIES_VALUE reference.

signalAsParameter.title: Signal Parameter
signalAsParameter.description: A boolean input accepted by entry, exit, and logical signal methods. It can be a signal object or a literal true or false.

items.title: Items
items.description: Signal list to combine. ALL_OF requires every item to be true. ANY_OF requires at least one item to be true.

compositeConditionSignal.title: Composite Signal
compositeConditionSignal.description: Combines one or more signals into a grouped condition using ALL_OF or ANY_OF.

method.title: Comparison Method
method.description: Comparison operator applied to two numeric inputs.

target.title: Target
target.description: Left-hand numeric input being compared. For AI-generated strategies, prefer SERIES_VALUE when referring to a previously defined named series. Raw quote shorthand like "CLOSE" is fine for direct market inputs. Avoid repeating complex inline indicator expressions here if they can be defined once in top-level 'series' and reused.

threshold.title: Threshold
threshold.description: Right-hand numeric input used as the comparison reference. This can be a constant number, raw quote shorthand, another indicator output, or SERIES_VALUE. Prefer constants for fixed thresholds such as 30, 50, or 70. Prefer SERIES_VALUE when the threshold comes from another named series.

params.title: Parameters
params.description:
Parameters for a numeric comparison signal.

Authoring preference (IMPORTANT for AI generation):
1. Prefer SERIES_VALUE when either side refers to a named series.
2. Prefer plain numbers for fixed thresholds.
3. Prefer quote shorthand only for direct OHLCV inputs.
4. Prefer reusing named series instead of repeating inline indicator expressions.

Discouraged but valid:
- Comparing a complex inline indicator on one side when the same logic is reused elsewhere.
- Mixing shorthand and nested styles inconsistently across the same strategy.

comparisonSignal.title: Comparison Signal
comparisonSignal.description: Boolean signal that compares two numeric values or series over time. Use this for threshold logic such as RSI < 30, CLOSE > EMA, or one named series compared to another.

method.title: Cross Method
method.description: Detects when one numeric series crosses above or below another.

value.title: Value
value.description: The changing numeric series being tested for a cross. For AI-generated strategies, this should usually be a price source, an indicator output, or SERIES_VALUE referring to a named series.

baseline.title: Baseline
baseline.description: The numeric reference that 'value' is checked against. This is commonly another named indicator output via SERIES_VALUE, a raw quote series, or a constant threshold.

params.title: Parameters
params.description:
Parameters for a cross signal.

Authoring preference (IMPORTANT for AI generation):
1. Prefer SERIES_VALUE when crossing one named series against another named series.
2. Prefer quote shorthand only for direct OHLCV inputs.
3. Prefer defining reusable indicators in top-level 'series' and then crossing their SERIES_VALUE outputs.
4. Use constants only when intentionally crossing against a fixed level.

Discouraged but valid:
- Repeating inline indicator expressions inside CROSSOVER or CROSSUNDER when they could be named once and reused.
- Using SERIES_NODE here. Cross signals operate on numeric values, not series definitions.

crossSignal.title: Cross Signal
crossSignal.description: Boolean signal that detects when one numeric series crosses above or below another numeric series or level.

binaryLogicalSignal.title: Binary Logical Signal
binaryLogicalSignal.description: Combines two signals with AND, OR, or XOR.

unaryLogicalSignal.title: Unary Logical Signal
unaryLogicalSignal.description: Applies NOT to a single signal.

signals.title: Signals
signals.description: Any valid Pludux signal object. A signal produces boolean values over time and is commonly used for entries, exits, and filters.

namedColor.title: Named Color
namedColor.description: CSS-style named color that can be used for plot styling.

hexColor.title: Hex Color
hexColor.description: Color in CSS hex format, such as #ff0000 or #0f0.

plotColor.title: Plot Color
plotColor.description: Color for a plot item. Can be a named color or an explicit RGBA color object.

plotSource.title: Plot Source
plotSource.description: Source used by a plot item. This can be a series or a signal result that Pludux can render.

name.title: Series Name
name.description: Name of the top-level series to plot.

seriesPlotSource.title: Series Plot Source
seriesPlotSource.description: Plot source that reads the output of a named series by name.

constantPlotSource.title: Constant Plot Source
constantPlotSource.description: Plot source that renders a constant numeric value.

source.title: Source
source.description: Series or signal source to render.

color.title: Color
color.description: Optional color override for this plot item.

linePlot.title: Line Plot
linePlot.description: Plots a source as a line.

level.title: Level
level.description: Y-axis value where the horizontal line is drawn.

color.title: Color
color.description: Optional color override for this plot item.

hlinePlot.title: Horizontal Line Plot
hlinePlot.description: Plots a constant horizontal line at a numeric level.

source.title: Source
source.description: Series or signal source to render.

color.title: Color
color.description: Optional color override for this plot item.

histogramPlot.title: Histogram Plot
histogramPlot.description: Plots a source as histogram bars.

label.title: Label
label.description: Optional label shown for the plot group.

overlay.title: Overlay
overlay.description: When true, render the plot group on top of the main price chart. When false, render it in a separate pane.

items.title: Items
items.description: Plot items included in this group.

plotGroup.title: Plot Group
plotGroup.description: A group of one or more plot items, optionally overlaid on price.

description: Schema for a Pludux backtest strategy file. This schema is designed to be machine-validatable and AI-friendly so an AI can generate complete strategy JSON files with named series, signals, trading rules, and optional chart plots. Important rule: use SERIES_NODE when another method needs a named series definition itself, and use SERIES_VALUE when another method needs the evaluated numeric output of a named series.
